description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical body composed of vertical wooden staves, bound together by two prominent rusted metal hoops—one near the top and one near the bottom. The wood has a natural, weathered appearance, with visible grain and subtle color variations, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and constructed from the same wood as the sides, tightly fitted to prevent leakage. The metal bands are slightly corroded, giving the barrel an authentic, utilitarian look typical of storage containers found in urban or industrial settings. There are no visible handles, spigots, or decorative markings, emphasizing its functional design. The overall build suggests it is intended for storage or transport of goods, possibly liquids or dry materials.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with rusted metal hoops and a flat, sealed top and bottom, showing no handles or markings. Its utilitarian design and aged materials suggest use for storage or transport in urban or industrial environments.
description_view_0: The barrel is seen from a slightly elevated angle, highlighting the flat wooden top and the upper metal hoop.
description_view_1: This view shows the front side of the barrel, with the wood grain and both metal hoops clearly visible.
description_view_2: The barrel's side is visible, showing the curvature of the staves and the texture of the wood and metal bands.
description_view_3: A direct side view, emphasizing the symmetry of the barrel and the even placement of the metal hoops.
